patterns.description: Denial letter omits some or all legally required ERISA disclosure rights
patterns.source: DOL EBSA Technical Release 2011-01; common in Aetna pre-2024 templates

patterns.description: Denial names a reviewing physician but lists no specialty, credentials, or license number. Filer can cross-check the named physician against state medical board records to verify specialty mismatch or implausible review volume.
patterns.source: ERISA 29 U.S.C. § 1133; MHPAEA Final Rule 2024 (29 CFR § 2590.712); Hansens v. Cigna (D. Conn. 2023)
realistic_flaws: reviewer listed by name only, no specialty or credentials
reviewer specialty does not match the clinical area of the denied service
patterns.appeal_vector: Invoke ERISA right to reviewer credentials; request the reviewing physician's name, specialty, license number, and time spent on this case. If credentials are missing or specialty mismatched, the denial review was procedurally defective.
